recipes.title: High Protein Pesto Pasta Recipe
recipes.description: This vegan spinach & artichoke spirulina pesto pasta recipe makes a zesty and flavorful pasta dish incorporating function and flavor by utilizing the superfood spirulina in a freshly prepared pesto.
ingredients: High Protein pasta made from chickpea, peas, or lentils
½ Cup Sun-dried tomatoes, packed in oil, drained
For the Pesto: (makes about 1 ½ cups)
1 Cup Packed parsley leaves, stems on!
⅓ Cup Olive oil or avocado oil (less bitter)
instructions: Prepare Pasta According to Packaged Instructions, remember to reserve about ¼ cup of the pasta water. Return the empty pot to the stove and set cooked pasta aside.
For the Pesto: In the base of a food processor add all of the ingredients. Pulse until blended thoroughly, add additional lemon juice or apple cider vinegar if needed for consistency.

Preheat the empty pasta pot over medium heat. Add the artichokes and Sundried tomatoes. Cook for 2 minutes. Add the Spinach and cook slightly, about 1-2 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add the cooked pasta, pesto, and pasta water, stir, and plate.
Garnish with vegan parmesan or toasted sunflower seeds if desired.

recipes.title: Homemade Vegetarian Chili Recipe
recipes.description: A delicious high protein, one-pot vegetarian chili recipe made with beyond meat burgers and simple ingredients simmered in hearty blend of seasonings.
ingredients: 1/2 cup roasted red pepper, drained from jar
1 can kidney beans, drained and rinsed
4 tablespoons orange juice or apple cider vinegar
instructions: In a saucepot on medium-high heat, add avocado oil or olive oil, minced garlic, diced onion, celery, and carrot. Stirring occasionally cook for 3 to 5 minutes or until the onions begin to brown.

Once the meat and vegetables have browned, add in the spices and roasted red peppers and cook for 1 minute until aromatic and stir to combine. Stir in the crumbled Trifecta meat patties and cook for 3 minutes or add 16 ounces of beyond meat and cook for 5 minutes.

Continue to cook on medium-high heat and add vegetable stock to release any of the brown bits stuck to the pan, then stir in the diced tomatoes and beans; bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer cook for 10 minutes. Season with orange juice or apple cider vinegar, a pinch of salt or pepper is desired then dig in!

recipes.title: Keto Mashed Cauliflower
recipes.description: Keto Mashed Cauliflower is a perfect side dish throughout the year, but also a great side for your holiday meals, like Thanksgiving and/or Christmas. Take your cauliflower sides to any event so that you can also enjoy all the celebration and sharing with your loved ones while staying keto!
ingredients: 6.5 cups/800g Cauliflower, Cut into Very Small Florets
0.5 cup/56.5 g Cheddar Cheese, Shredded + 0.25 cup/28.25 g to top off and broil
instructions: Preheat oven to 420° F.

Cut cauliflower to very small floret size, enough to imitate the size/texture of macaroni pasta. Place in a foil lined sheet tray and drizzle with garlic powder, salt, 2 Tsp. pepper, and oil; toss gently to coat. Bake, for 12-15 minutes, until tender. Check constantly to avoid florets from burning. Remove once golden brown.

Cut bacon strips half lengthwise, and then widthwise to small rectangular strips. Add bacon to a sauté pan, and bring the heat up from cold to medium. The bacon will begin rendering and cooking slowly. Don't stir for some time, until bacon begins to crisp up. Once the bacon begins looking like crispy small teeth, stir with a wooden utensil, and remove from heat once fully crispy. Keep aside in a plate lined with paper towels. This will absorb the excess fat. Feel free to use some of the fat a...

In a medium saucepan, combine cream, nutritional yeast, cheese, and remaining 2 Tsp. black pepper and 2 Tsp. of garlic powder. Over medium heat, and constantly stirring, heat up mixture while the cheese melts and incorporates. Mix often with a whisk. Once fully melted, reduce heat to low and keep warm, stirring every once in a while until ready. If the mixture is too thick, add extra milk.

Switch oven to broil. Once cauliflower is ready, transfer to a baking dish or a shallow oven-safe baking dish, enough to hold the cauliflower and the sauce together. Combine the former with the bacon, and the cheese sauce. Add 1/4 cup of cheese on top and broil until cheese is golden brown and crispy on top. Feel free to add chives or green onion bulbs for garnish. Serve warm.

recipes.title: Low-Carb Portobello Mushroom Bruschetta
recipes.description: Whether you're looking to get more vegetables into your diet or in the search for a tasty healthy appetizer, this recipe is the perfect thing for you!
instructions: First, heat up a skillet or a grill. Then spray the Mushrooms with non stick spray. Grill for 2-3 minutes on each side. Remove from heat but keep the stove/ grill on.
In a bowl, mix the tomatoes, garlic, balsamic vinegar, and basil together. Add the mix to the inside of the mushrooms.
Top the mushrooms with cheese and place back on the skillet/grill.
Cover and heat for 2-3 minutes or until the cheese has melted. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Paleo Gluten-Free Granola Recipe
recipes.description: This succulent, nutty, and texture-rich granola is an incredible paleo-approved and gluten-free granola recipe you can make in bulk and use as needed.
instructions: Preheat oven to 300F. Measure cashews, macadamia nuts and almonds into a food processor. Pulse until the nuts are rough chopped, 3-5 times. Transfer to a bowl. In the same bowl, add pepitas chia seeds, crancherry, coconut flakes, cinnamon, allspice, and cayenne pepper.
In a separate bowl, combine melted or liquid coconut oil, maple syrup, vanilla extract, and almond flour. Mix to combine.
Combine nut/seed mix with coconut oil/almond flour mix. Mix to fully coat all ingredients together.

Transfer paleo granola to a parchment-lined baking sheet and spread evenly. Bake for 20 minutes in a preheated oven. Remove from the oven after time is up, let cool completely and then break into small pieces. Transfer to an airtight container.

recipes.title: Simple Vegetable Frittata Recipe
recipes.description: Make this savory vegetable egg white frittata in advance to add some lean protein and nutrition to your weekly meal prep.
instructions: Pre-heat oven to 350 degrees. Warm 9-inch casserole dish in the oven before cooking.
Warm oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the spinach and turn off the heat.
In a blender, add egg whites and cottage cheese. Pulse on high until the mixture is fully combined.
Remove the casserole dish from the oven. Add veggies to the casserole dish and spread evenly.
Pour egg mixture over the veggies.
Cook for 45 min to 1 hour until the center is cooked through. Let cool for 15 minutes before serving.

recipes.title: Smoked Paprika Steak Stir Fry Recipe
recipes.description: The perfect match for any meal prep diet. This paleo and keto steak stir-fry recipe is smokey, savory and a little tangy, and takes less than 10 minutes to make!
instructions: Wash and chop your veggies and slice the steak into bite-sized pieces. Mince the fresh cilantro leaves and stems.
Using spray oil, coat a non-stick skillet and saute asparagus over medium-high heat for 2 to 3 minutes.
Add tomatoes and cook for another 2 minutes.
Add steak and cook for an additional minute, then turn off the heat.
Add sauce mixture to the pan and stir to coat everything evenly.
Plate it and dig in!

recipes.title: Trifecta Five Minute Burger Recipe
recipes.description: Looking for a simple quick meal using Trifecta's beef patties? Here's a legitimately simple and quick recipe. Enjoy and indulge in a healthy burger recipe!
ingredients: 4 hamburger buns, whole wheat or whole grain, toasted
2 tablespoons chipotle mayo or favorite condiment
½ cup arugula or any other bitter leafy green
½ cup Trifecta portobello mushroom, warmed
1 cup favorite Trifecta veggies, as a side
instructions: Begin by toasting buns in a toaster or toaster oven until golden brown.

Microwave Trifecta patties for 2 minutes to reheat, by making a small hole in a corner to allow steam to escape. Carefully remove plastic film and keep warm until needed. Spread the chipotle mayo on both burger buns. On the bottom bun, add a small ⅛ packed cup of arugula.

Slice tomatoes. Slice the avocados lengthwise and separate them into quarters. Fan avocado and place on top of the leafy greens. Carefully transfer the patties from the microwave to the bottom burger bun. Place 3 thin tomato slices or 2 thick slices on top of each patty. Top with ⅛ cup warm mushrooms and place the top bun on the burger.
Optional side: Reheat 1 cup of your favorite Trifecta vegetable and enjoy your 5-minute dinner!

recipes.title: Turkey Stuffed Bell Peppers
recipes.description: The combination of both sweet and spicy coming from the bell peppers, mixed with the tender ground turkey, and the gooey goodness of melted cheese, will certainly make this recipe a favorite!
ingredients: 2 Whole Bell Peppers (We used Red & Green)
1/2 Cup of Low Fat Shredded Cheddar Cheese
instructions: Preheat oven to 325 degrees. Then, cut the bell peppers in half, remove seeds, and clean.

In a large bowl, crumble the Trifecta turkey burgers and mix with the onions, bread crumbs, tomato, garlic, and other seasoning. Add half of the cheese to the bowl (save other half for the top). Add a dash of salt and pepper. Mix well together.
Spray a baking sheet with non-stick cooking spray. Then add the bell pepper halves. Fill each bell pepper with the stuffing. Heat for 15-20 minutes.
Remove from heat and add the rest of the cheese to the top of the peppers. Place back in the oven for 3-5 minutes or until cheese is melted.

recipes.title: Ultimate Vegan Pot Roast Recipe
recipes.description: Fall in love with this easy and wholesome plant-based vegan pot roast recipe with Beyond Sausage® Brat and explosive flavor.
ingredients: 1 package Beyond Beef® Brat Sausage* (4 links)
2 ¼ cup vegetable stock or broth, low-sodium
instructions: Heat pot over medium-high heat and add oil. Sear the fresh portabello mushrooms for 5 minutes until they begin to dark in color and shrink in size. Add 1/4 cup water to help steam them and prevent burning.
Remove the mushrooms from the pan. still cooking over medium-high heat add the sliced Beyond Sausage® and saute for 3-4 minutes.
Add your carrots, onions, and celeriac and cook for 5 minutes, until slightly browned.
Carefully deglaze the pot by adding the pomegranate juice. Use this to scrape off the 'fond' from the bottom and sides of the pot.
Now add the spices, mustard, stock, and mushrooms and simmer for 25-30 minutes until the mushrooms are tender and the liquid has reduced more than a quarter of its original volume.
Finish by thickening the braising liquid with cornstarch or arrowroot starch and dig in!

recipes.title: Vegan Southwest Baked Sweet Potato Recipe
recipes.description: Our vegan Southwest baked sweet potato recipe only requires a few fresh ingredients, spices, and a baked sweet potato for a satisfyingly simple meal.
ingredients: Trifecta A la Carte Beyond Meat® burgers,
crumbled or 1 (16 oz) package Beyond Meat burgers®
¼ cup vegetable stock or water
1 Roma tomato, diced or ½ cup pico de gallo
instructions: To bake your potato in less time, cut your sweet potatoes in half lengthwise and rub with oil (utilize coconut oil for extra flavor), place sweet potatoes cut side down on a baking sheet lined with foil and roast for 25-30 minutes until tender. Move onto the next steps while your sweet potatoes are baking in the oven.
In a food processor or blender, add the cilantro, vegan yogurt, lime juice, and chili powder. Blend until smooth, then place crema into a separate container and store in the fridge until needed.

To prepare your filling, heat a large saute pan over medium-high heat and add 1 teaspoon oil, then add the garlic and onion. Saute the garlic and onion for 4 minutes until they are fragrant and begin to brown.
Crumble the Beyond Meat or Trifecta A la Carte Beyond Meat burger patties into the saute pan and cook for 5 minutes, mixing with the onion and garlic

Add the corn, bell pepper, and spices, continuing to cook over medium-high heat for 2 more minutes then finish by adding lime juice and stock or water. Cook for an additional minute then remove from heat.
Build your sweet potato by layering the filling into the baked potato, then topping with sliced green onion, avocado, and the cilantro crema.

recipes.title: Blackened Salmon Tacos Recipe
recipes.description: The combination of the red cabbage-avocado salad, the spicy aioli, and the blackened Trifecta salmon, make this blackened salmon tacos recipe incredibly tasty.
ingredients: 160g or 3 egg, whites, and yolks separated
instructions: Preheat oven (or a small appliance oven) to 425F. Remove 4 Trifecta salmon filet from container and set on top of a cutting board.
Mix 3 tablespoons oil plus all spices in a small bowl. Add a pinch of salt and pepper. Mix. A paste should form. Brush all sides of the salmon filets using the entire cajun spice paste.

Transfer to a foil-lined baking sheet and place in the oven for 5 minutes to heat through. After 5 min, change settings in the oven to broil or toast. This will ‘blacken’ the salmon. Broil for 1 minute. Keep an eye on the oven so that it doesn’t excessively charr the spice on the fish or smoke up your kitchen. Remove from oven once done and keep warm.

As fish blackens, slice the red cabbage and diced avocado. Add together in a bowl and gently mix together. Splash with a pinch of salt, olive oil, and lemon juice. Mix all ingredients for aioli in a separate small bowl and keep on the side until needed. If you have one, transfer aioli to a squeeze bottle.
Reheat tortillas on an open flame or in the microwave for 30-45 seconds. If reheating over an open flame, turn constantly until both sides are heated through. Keep warm until needed.

To plate: Place two tortillas on a plate. At the bottom of each tortilla, add a small amount of the aioli, followed by a small amount of the avocado-cabbage mix. Gently and carefully pull apart each salmon filet with your hands dividing one filet into two tortillas. Add the cabbage mix and a spoonful of the creamy aioli on top.
Garnish with fresh herbs, sliced radish, and serve with a lime wedge.

recipes.title: Chicken Burrito Bowl Recipe
recipes.description: Instead of going out to eat and pay an excessive amount of money for a delicious bowl you can make at home, find a super simple and quick recipe here for any day of the week.
ingredients: Fresh cilantro stems and or cheese of choice
instructions: Dice the chicken into even small squares, bite-size. Keep in a bowl. Chop the lettuce and keep separated. Make sure you clean your cutting board between tasks to avoid cross-contamination of flavors.
Mix the marinade ingredients in a bowl and whisk to incorporate. A paste should form. Toss the diced chicken into the bowl and coat uniformly.
Spray a non-stick pan with spray oil and heat over medium heat. Once the pan is beginning to slightly smoke, add the chicken and heat up 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly. Keep warm.
Meanwhile, reheat your rice in two separate bowls for 2.5 minutes in the microwave.

In a plate or bowl, plate ⅓ cup of white rice next to ⅓ cup of brown rice. Weight 4.5 ounces of chicken per bowl, ¼ cup of pico de gallo, ¼ cup of romaine, ¼ fanned avocado, and 2 teaspoons of greek yogurt on top. Pick washed fresh cilantro and garnish.

recipes.title: Easy Chicken Tacos Pibil Recipe
recipes.description: An adjusted recipe, yet highly delicious combination of spices to imitate the authentic cochinita pibil recipe, but made quick and simple!
ingredients: 16 ounces Trifecta chicken breast, diced or Trifecta pulled chicken
1 cup Cabbage slaw, store-bought (¼ cup divided between all tortillas)
instructions: Mix all spices in a bowl along with orange juice and olive oil.
warm up the chicken in the microwave for 2-3 minutes or in a saute pan and toss with the marinade, set aside to keep warm while heating the tortillas on the stovetop.
Prepare the slaw in a separate bowl by tossing the cabbage with a squeeze of lime and a pinch of salt
build the tacos by layering the warmed and marinated chicken with the sale and enjoy.

recipes.title: Fall Pomegranate Chicken Salad Recipe
recipes.description: This super beautiful, simple, healthy and delicious salad is a great option for lunch, using in-season fall and winter ingredients!
ingredients: 16 ounces Trifecta chicken breast, diced into squares evenly
4 tablespoons pomegranate seeds, store-bought to save time
¼ cup Trifecta almonds, favorite kind
4 tablespoon Mustard Honey Vin, store-bought or home-made
instructions: Prepare all ingredients as indicated. In a big bowl, mix all greens together. Season with a sprinkle of kosher salt.

Portion 0.5 cup of green mix in each plate, ⅓ cup of quinoa, 4 ounces of diced chicken, sprinkle 1 tablespoon of pom seeds, followed by 1 tablespoon of almonds, and 1 tablespoon of crumbled feta cheese in the middle of the salad.
Dress the salad with 1 tablespoon of mustard honey vin. Taste for seasoning and add another sprinkle of salt to the salad to bring up the flavor of all the ingredients. Enjoy!
Conversely, feel free to mix each salad in a bowl following the same serving sizes.

recipes.title: High-Protein Pasta Salad Recipe
recipes.description: A tasty high-protein pasta salad recipe topped with Trifecta A La Carte shrimp packs 46 grams of protein per serving!
ingredients: 1/4 cup shredded manchego cheese, parmesan, or feta (optional)
Pinch salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Cook your high-protein pasta according to package instructions and then gather all of the ingredients for the pasta salad. Combine cooked pasta and all of the ingredients in a bowl, massaging together to create pasta salad.
In a small bowl combine shrimp and all of the ingredients besides the avocado oil. Serve it warm by sautéing it in a pan for 2 minutes with the avocado oil.
Top your pasta salad with the shrimp, cheese, and any other toppings you'd like! Portion out this recipe into 4 meal prep containers each with 4 oz of shrimp.

recipes.title: New-England Style Shrimp Rolls
recipes.description: A slightly-sweet, buttery delight, this shrimp roll will change everything you know about sandwiches. With only a few ingredients and some fresh produce - this recipe can be made in a matter of minutes!
ingredients: 1 Tsp of Paprika (can use more to top)
instructions: First, heat the Trifecta shrimp up in the microwave for 2-3 minutes or until warm.
Then spray the potato bun with the butter and toast.
On a cutting board, dice up the celery and cut the green onions.
In a bowl, cut up the shrimp as best as you can into smaller pieces. Then add all the ingredients (except the green onions) and mix together. Include a dash of salt and pepper.
Fill the potato bun with the shrimp mixture. Lightly top with paprika and green onions. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Simple Quinoa Bowl Recipe
recipes.description: Crush your vegan macros with this easy grain salad recipe. It's crunchy, vibrant, a little sweet, and somewhat cheesy - and more than enough to keep you feeling satisfied.
instructions: Warm quinoa in a microwave for about 1 minute, until lightly warmed (close to room temperature). Add to a large mixing bowl, stirring to break it up.
Drain broccoli and add to the bowl. Chop any large pieces into smaller, bite-sized pieces.
Coarsely chop almonds and add to the bowl.
Chop scallions and parsley and add to quinoa.
Chop figs into small pieces and add to the mixture.
Season with olive oil and lemon juice.
Add salt and pepper to taste.
Finish with nutritional yeast and enjoy!

recipes.title: Simple Salmon Salad Recipe
recipes.description: Perfect your keto or paleo meal prep in minutes with this easy and healthy salmon salad recipe. Get 30 grams of protein and only 7 grams of net carbs, with a ton of flavor and nutrition.
instructions: Mince shallot and zest lemon.
In a small bowl mix lemon zest, lemon juice, shallot, and dijon mustard. Whisk in olive oil until smooth. Season with salt and pepper to taste and set aside.
Wash and chop lettuce and tomatoes. Set aside.
Cook bacon until crispy and let cool. Once cooled, chop into bite-sized pieces.
Peel and cube avocado.
Divide ingredients into four portions and toss together lettuce, tomato, bacon, and avocado.
Pan sear or warm salmon in the oven for a few minutes.
Toss salad greens in dressing and top with warmed salmon.
Enjoy!

recipes.title: The Best Avocado Ahi Tuna Salad
recipes.description: A perfect balance between creamy and crunchy, this keto tuna salad is absolutely delicious! Perfect for a lighter summer dip option, or even as the base of a sandwich, this mouthwatering mixture is full of healthy-fats and micronutrients.
ingredients: 1/2 Cup of Diced Yellow Bell Peppers
instructions: In a medium-size bowl mash up the ahi tuna. Then add the avocado and mix.
Add in the tomatoes, bell peppers, onions, and lemon juice. Mix.
Serve with bread of choice, or use this as a base for lettuce wraps!

recipes.title: Vegan Savory Grilled Tofu, Broccoli, and Rice Bowl
recipes.description: Organic tofu seasoned with nutty and savoury nutritional yeast, grilled to perfection and served on top a bed of brown rice, black beans, and fresh veggies, tossed in a simple vinaigrette. Packed with plant-based protein and good nutrition.
ingredients: 1 can black beans, drained and rinsed
instructions: Evenly slice your tofu into strips (about 2 ounces each), pat dry and season with half of the nutritional yeast and pepper.
Add cooking spray on a non-stick grill pan and warm to medium-high heat. Once ready, add your tofu and grill for 3 minutes on each side until it starts to crisp up and leave grill marks.
Add your rice, drained beans, and broccoli to a large mixing bowl. Season with oil, remaining nutritional yeast, black pepper, and salt.
Portion out your rice mixture onto four plates or meal prep containers and top each with 3 pieces of grilled tofu.
Garnish with herbs and a sprinkle of salt.
Enjoy!

recipes.title: Vegan Tofu Tabbouleh Bowl Recipe
recipes.description: Dig into this phytonutrient dense vegan tofu tabbouleh bowl recipe is composed of fried tofu, quinoa, and fresh vegetables tossed with a refreshing almond and walnut tabbouleh
ingredients: 3-4 small beets, roasted at home, or store-bought sliced
or 1 package of firm tofu cut into cubes
instructions: Prepare your fried tofu, tabbouleh, and quinoa according to recipe directions. Combine the quinoa and 2 cups tabbouleh together to marinade. You should have extra tabbouleh for finishing your bowl.

Wash your vegetables and greens. Remove the stems from the kale and finely chop. Using a vegetable peeler, peel the carrot into fine strips. Toss the kale, carrots, and chickpeas together and set aside.
Remove the peel from your orange and slice into slices. Do this over a bowl to save any orange juice and mix it into the tabbouleh.
To build your bowl: measure 1/3 cup tabbouleh and quinoa mixture, 4 ounces tofu, 1/2 cup roasted beets, 3-4 sliced orange, 1 cup kale, carrot, and chickpea salad. Top with extra tabbouleh if desired.

recipes.title: 10 Minute Shrimp Stir-Fry With Noodles Recipe
recipes.description: This 10-minute sweet and spicy shrimp stir-fry with noodles recipe is a delicious recipe to add some variety to your week and meal prep!
ingredients: 1 cup of red bell pepper, sliced thin
1 package pad Thai noodles or noodle of choice
instructions: Prepare your noodles according to the packaged instructions, but cook for 1-2 minutes less to keep them more firm, then shock the noodles in cold water to stop the cooking process. Combine all the ingredients for the sauce in a small bowl.
Choose your cookware and heat over medium-heat

Heat your pan for 1-2 minutes before adding in the oil, then add in any raw or uncooked vegetables, in this case the snap peas and red bell peppers. Stir fry for 2 minutes, then add the Trifecta broccoli and mushrooms, cook for 2 more minutes. Remember to stir throughout the process, this is a high-heat cooking method. Add the shrimp and stir-fry for 3 minutes then add the sauce and stir till all the ingredients are coated evenly.

recipes.title: 2 Minute Protein Molten Chocolate Lava Cake
recipes.description: Prepare a nutrient-dense chocolate lava cake in less than 3 minutes, perfect for a sweet snack or protein-packed dessert.
ingredients: 1 tablespoon almond butter or peanut butter powder
instructions: Gather and measure all of your ingredients for your molten chocolate lava cake.

In a large bowl whisk together all of the ingredients for your cake until you form a batter. Coat two ramekins or coffee mugs with non-stick spray and divide the batter evenly in half between the two.
Microwave your molten lava chocolate cake on high for 1 minute and 15 seconds, to ensure the molten lava and gooey center! Then enjoy!

recipes.title: 20 Minute Thai Beef Rice Bowl Recipe
recipes.description: Inspired in Thai flavors, this versatile quick bowl recipe is set to satisfy your hunger cravings and meet your caloric needs!
ingredients: 16 ounces or 4 Trifecta beef patties, crumbled
3 tablespoons low sodium soy sauce, low sodium tamari, or liquid aminos
instructions: In a saucepan, quickly bring fish sauce, sesame oil, honey, lime juice, low sodium tamari/soy sauce, and sambal oelek up to a boil, reduce to simmer slightly, and mix to incorporate all ingredients. All ingredients should come together in 3 minutes tops. If the sauce is too loose, simmer for an extra 2 minutes, but avoid simmering down the sauce excessively to a thick syrup. We want a sauce that will coat the protein evenly. Think of the body of running maple, just slightly loser in the body.
Crumble the beef patties with your hands and add them to the saucepan with the simmering sauce. Add the carrots and Trifecta broccoli as well.
Cook for 3 minutes or until completely warmed through. Keep warm, avoiding the bottom from burning and adjusting heat as needed. If sauce becomes too thick, add a splash of stock or water.
Warm rice and divide into four bowls by plating ⅓ cup on each.

Plate 4-5 ounces of beef and sauce mixture on each plate. You may have a residual beef mix, as the volume increases when carrots and broccoli are added. Distribute amongst the four bowls evenly. Garnish with sesame seeds, chives, and scallions if desired!

recipes.title: BBQ Chicken Sweet Potato Toast
recipes.description: Sweet and savory, this bbq chicken sweet potato toast is perfect as an appetizer or meal! Enjoy for a special occasion or as a fun new weekly entree.
instructions: First, Heat the sweet potatoes up in the microwave for about 5 minutes (each potato).
Using two forks try and shred the chicken up as much as possible. Also, dice the onions.
In a bowl, mix together the chicken, onions, and BBQ Sauce.

Once the potatoes have cooled. Cut into strips (hot dog style) to use as the base of the toast. Using a skillet, grill the sweet potatoes until golden brown. Add the mozzarella cheese to the top of the toast and let melt.
Add the chicken/onion mixture to the top of the toast.
Top with fresh cilantro. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Bacon and Guacamole Deviled Egg Recipe
recipes.description: Add healthy fats and flavor to a classic recipe with guacamole and crispy bacon deviled eggs. The perfect high protein snack or party appetizer.
ingredients: To make this recipe you'll need:
1/2 teaspoon smoked paprika or chili powder
instructions: Scoop the yolks out of the egg halves and place half in a medium sized mixing bowl. Discard the remaining or save for later.
Mash the yolks with a fork and mix with the avocado and yogurt until smooth.
Adding yogurt help keeps the guacamole from turning brown!
Scoop the seeds out of the jalapeno and dice into small piece. Setting aside 1 tbsp. Discard the rest or save it for another recipe. Feel free to add a bit more to increase the spiciness.
Wash and finely chop the cilantro, including some of the stems. Set aside some of the leaves for garnish.
Dice the diced onion and set aside 1 tbsp for mixing.
Add the jalapeno, onion, and cilantro to the avocado mixture and stir to combine. Add lime and s
eason with salt to taste.
Place about one tablespoon of the mixture back into the holes left by the yolks in the eggs and serve garnished with bacon and a pinch of smoked paprika. Garnish with cilantro!
Bacon and Guacamole Deviled Eggs
COOK TIME:
5 min
PREP TIME:
5 min
YIELD:

recipes.title: Baked Chicken Parmesan with Zucchini Pasta
recipes.description: This recipes is a great way to save some carbs and enjoy a filling meal. Eat it as the main course and pair with a nice salad, it's sure to satisfy any foodie.
instructions: First, heat the oven to 425. Then spray a pan with spray butter. In a bowl mix the bread crumbs and Italian seasoning.
Take your chicken breast and spray it with butter and then dip in the bread crumbs.
Bake the chicken for 8 minutes on each side.
Remove the chicken from the oven when crispy golden. Top with reduced-fat parmesan cheese.
Heat the zucchini noodles in the microwave for 3-5 minutes.
On a plate, serve the noodles with the chicken parmesan and add the spaghetti sauce. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Baked Egg Bites Recipe
recipes.description: Our baked egg bites recipe makes the perfect breakfast or snack option to satisfy your meal prep needs. Add your favorite veggies for a variety of bites!
ingredients: 5 oz or 144g cottage cheese, full fat (or you can also use ricotta cheese)
Dash of Tabasco (trust me on this one)
instructions: Preheat oven to 375F.

In a large bowl, mix whole eggs, egg whites, cottage cheese, salt, pepper, oil, and tabasco. Add all ingredients in a blender and bland for 30 seconds until fully incorporated. Blend in batches if needed.
Using a muffin pan, liberally coat each muffin mold with coconut oil spray. Coat all sides by spreading the oil with a paper towel, then spray one more time.
In a separate, small bowl, weigh out 50g of the egg mix and transfer to the muffin mold. Repeat until all the egg mix is portioned.

Add about 1/2" of water to a baking sheet and place the muffin pan inside the water-filled baking sheet. Bake in the preheated oven for 26 minutes, rotating the pan halfway through the cooking process. Set a timer.

Once the time is up, check the doneness by poking a couple of the egg bites with a toothpick or a cake tester. No egg should stick to it. If the bites need a little extra cooking, add 2 minutes at a time. Remove from the oven and use a butter knife or offset spatula to scrape the sides of each muffin pan.
Transfer to a wire rack and let cool. Store in an airtight container in the fridge. Stack them but don't squish.

recipes.title: Beef Enchilada Recipe
recipes.description: A few ingredients make up for a quick dinner for any day of the week. This classic Mexican dish is set to be a quick solution for any day of the week.
ingredients: 16 each corn or flour tortillas, soft 4”-6”
2 cups enchilada sauce, store-bought or preferred enchilada sauce
instructions: Preheat oven to 350 F. Warm up the tortillas by either using the microwave and reheat them wrapped in napkin cloths for about a minute and a half at 75% of the microwave’s power, or by reheating them on an open flame or on a hot non-stick pan, quickly turning the tortillas on each side to warm them through and keeping them warm in a small bundle.

Microwave the Trifecta beef patties covered completely by a wet paper cloth towel, for a minute and a half. After the patties are slightly cooled down, crumble the patties to its original form - ground beef - with your hands into a bowl. Combine the Trifecta bell pepper with the beef and mix to incorporate. Keep aside.
Get a baking dish ready, by spraying a light coat of oil on all sides of the baking dish.

Set up a cutting board as your working station to roll the enchiladas. Grab the warm tortillas, beef and green bell pepper mix, enchilada sauce, and set up your station to be able to work in the following way:
A. Place one, two, or however many tortillas comfortably fit on your cutting board right next to each other, without overlapping.
B. Weigh and place 1.5 ounces of the beef+bell pepper mix on one end of the tortillas.

C. Roll the tortilla over the beef and create a semi-tight cylinder. Carefully transfer the tortillas to the sprayed baking sheet, and place the seam in the bottom to avoid the enchiladas from rolling open. Place them all back to back starting from one end of the baking sheet, to the other. Repeat with all 16 tortillas.

Pour the sauce on top of the enchiladas, and top with cheese. Place in the oven for about 7-10 minutes or until cheese is melted and enchiladas are warmed through. Carefully remove the pan from the oven and serve 4 enchiladas per plate. Garnish with cilantro. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Buffalo Chicken Meal Prep Recipe
recipes.description: This healthy buffalo recipe can be cross utilized for any meal prep need! Use this recipe as your guideline and have fun making any buffalo-inspired recipe
ingredients: ½ cup Frank’s Red Hot sauce
instructions: Melt the butter in a medium saucepan over medium-low heat. Once fully melted, add the rest of the ingredients in the pot. Mix, taste, and adjust for seasoning if needed (unlikely as Frank’s is pretty salty as is). Bring to a gentle boil and let sauce slightly reduced.

Dice chicken into even bite-sized squares. Transfer chicken into the pot with sauce and bring up to heat until chicken is fully heated through. Reduce heat to low and keep covered until needed. Stir every now and then if for some reason you keep the pot on the fire for longer than 5 minutes.
Meanwhile, heat up Trifecta mashed potato and cauliflower in two separate microwave-safe bowls for 3 minutes. Add 30 seconds if the food is not heated all the way through.

As an idea: Plate ½ cup of mashed potatoes in each plate, topped by 4 ounces of chicken buffalo and ½ cup of the heated cauliflower. Feel free to sprinkle any leftover buffalo sauce on the cauliflower.

recipes.title: Chicken Katsu Tacos Recipe
recipes.description: We've taken the typical Japanese chicken katsu and transformed it into a delicious and highly fun taco recipe for you to enjoy any day of the week!
ingredients: 3 (12 ounce total) Trifecta chicken breast, butterflied across (see image below)
1 tablespoon milk or half and half
1/2 cup cornstarch (or rice flour if desired)
¼ each or 1 cup red cabbage, thinly shredded
Kosher salt and freshly ground pepper
½ teaspoon oyster or fish sauce
instructions: Preheat oven to 200F for holding chicken later. In a heavy-bottomed high-sided skillet, heat the oil over low-medium heat until it reaches 350F. Keep a close eye on the oil to avoid burning it (use a high temp probe to measure the oil’s temperature). Reduce temp once the oil has reached the 350F.

Thinly slice the cabbage into a separate bowl. Add light mayo, rice vinegar, tabasco, cilantro, garlic powder, and season with salt and pepper. Mix to incorporate and fully coat the cabbage. Taste for seasoning and adjust if needed.
Mix all ingredients for tonkatsu sauce in a small serving bowl and set aside.

Crack eggs into a bowl and add the whole milk or cream. Beat together until fully mixed. Season lightly with salt and pepper. In a separate bowl, add panko crumbs. Add a pinch of salt. Mix to incorporate. Add cornstarch in a third bowl.

Butterfly the Trifecta chicken by cutting across through the middle, causing the chicken breast to open like a book or a butterfly. Since the chicken is cooked, the breading process will take one extra step.

First, coat the Trifecta chicken in the egg wash, then coat in the cornstarch/flour, once again in the egg wash, and lastly panko, shaking off any excess after each step. Set the coated chicken in a resting rack or cutting board before shallow frying. Repeat with all chicken.

Once oil temperature is 350F, carefully place the chicken in the oil. The best practice is to gently place the chicken breast in the oil pan, by nestling the chicken away from you into the oil, to avoid any potential hot oil splashes from flying your way.

Shallow fry for 1 minute per side until golden brown. Check color to make sure you're not burning the bread. Repeat for all 4 pieces of chicken. As the chickens are done, remove with tongs and transfer to paper towels to drain any excess fat. Keep warm in a small oven slightly warmed at 200F, or covered and kept in a warm area of the kitchen. Remove and slice when ready to serve. Meanwhile, warm up tortillas.

To plate: Place two tortillas in a plate, a small bed of slaw on the bottom of the tortilla, 1 sliced tonkatsu chicken breast divided into the two tortillas (4 oz per two tortillas), topped with ~¼ cup of cabbage slaw, and 2 teaspoons of the tonkatsu sauce drizzled on top. Add fresh cilantro and Fresno peppers as garnish if desired and available.

recipes.title: Chicken Pad Thai Recipe
recipes.description: Easy, quick, and delicious chicken pad Thai recipe using Trifecta A La Carte items to make your meal prep a breeze!
instructions: Cut Trifecta chicken into bite-sized squares, and gather all other needed ingredients.
Prepare noodles as indicated by the package. Drain and keep hot on the side.

Melt coconut oil in a pan over medium-high heat. Add carrots and broccoli in the pan. Sautee to warm through. Add chicken and saute to warm through. Lastly, add noodles along with pad thai sauce and mix to fully incorporate.
Separate into 4 bowls. Top off with chopped peanuts, bean sprouts, and the fresh-picked cilantro. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Chicken and Mushroom Sauced-Noodle Recipe
recipes.description: This chicken and mushroom sauced-noodle recipe is a heart-warming peanut-Thai based recipe that will satisfy any weekday meal prep need.
ingredients: 8 oz cooked Lo mein, ramen, or lamian noodles
Cilantro or Thai basil for garnish, optional
instructions: Cook the noodles of your choice according to the packaging instructions. Drain, rinse under cold water and return to the same pan. Keep warm on the side off the heat. I highly encourage you to cook your noodles al dente -- this will help offset any potential reheating you may do.

If you so desire, add the hoisin and Sriracha to a small saucepan. Conversely, do not add these two optional ingredients, and simply add the Thai-peanut sauce of your choice to a pan and begin reducing it while all the other ingredients cook. I suggest you start this process as you are dropping your noodles to cook since you want a nicely reduced sauce to coat all your noodles evenly.

In a large saute pan, add the oil and bring the heat up to medium-high. Once the oil is shimmering, cook the garlic for a quick 1 minute. Immediately add the chicken, and cook for 2 minutes, stirring often. Add a pinch of lime juice to deglaze the pan if the bottom is getting too brown. Add the mushroom, saute and then add the spinach. Stir often to help cook the spinach and continue to reheat all other items. Lastly, I also suggest adding a splash of the sauce to the pan to coat all of the i...

Combine the now reduced sauce with the noodles and bring to a light simmer to reheat the noodles and sauce them. Add a pinch of water to the sauce if it has reduced too much. The sauce should beautifully coat the noodles - if the sauce is too watery, reduce the sauce a bit further with the noodles in it. Avoid reducing the sauce excessively or cooking the noodles in the sauce for too long as this will overcook them.

In a bowl, combine 3 ounces of the sauced noodles, along with 5 ounces of the chicken-vegetable mix. Mix to incorporate. Be careful not to burn yourself. Transfer to a plate and serve. Add any extra vegetables as desired for color and extra fiber (I added cherry tomatoes).

recipes.title: Coconut Crusted Fish Tacos Recipe
recipes.description: A mix of savory and sweet, this coconut crusted fish tacos recipe is a delicious blend you won't be able to get enough of.
ingredients: 1/2 cup red cabbage, thinly sliced or shredded
instructions: Begin by making the salsa. Dice the mango, jicama, tomatoes, and cilantro and add to a bowl. Shred the cabbage and mix. Season with a hint of salt, black pepper, and lime juice if desired. Keep on the side. Slice jalapenos and keep on the side as well.
In a small bowl, mix the bread crumbs, red pepper flakes, and coconut flakes.
Remove Trifecta cod from the package and spray with cooking spray on both sides. Roll the cod in the bread crumb and mix until the cod is thoroughly covered and an even crust forms.

If using an air fryer, set the air fryer settings to cook fish fillets or to fry chicken wings - this setting will help develop the crust and get it golden brown. This might take anywhere between 5-10 minutes depending on the air fryer. Avoid burning the crust.
If using a skillet, add 2 tablespoons of oil to a small non-stick skillet and heat over medium heat. Heat until oil is slightly smoking and it begins to shimmer.

Once the oil is hot, pan-fry the cod for 1 minute on each side, adjusting the heat as needed. Ideally, pan-fry both cod filets at the same time. Remove and transfer to a resting rack, and keep warm until needed (keep warm in small appliance oven set to 'keep warm' settings).

Reheat the tortillas either on an open flame by constantly turning the tortilla over the flame until hot, or on a hot small non-stick skillet. Conversely, warm the tortillas in a microwave for 30 seconds. Wrap the tortillas in a damp small cloth towel or paper cloth towel. Keep warm.
Using the corn tortillas as a base, add the red cabbage and mango salsa, half the cod fillets to each tortilla, a splash of the mango salsa again, and jalapenos.
Top with cotija cheese or avocado, if desired. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Coconut Curry Chicken Bowl Recipe
recipes.description: This coconut-curry bowl is not only a quick meal prep recipe, but also a dish filled with massive amounts of flavor!
ingredients: 3 garlic cloves, or 1 teaspoon pre-minced garlic
½ cup Curry sauce, favorite, store-bought
2 cups Trifecta white or brown rice
instructions: Prepare Trifecta chicken as indicated and keep in a bowl until needed. Dice onion and garlic. Open coconut milk and the separate amounts needed.

In a pan, heat up coconut oil over medium-high until shimmering and slightly smoking. Immediately add the onion and garlic cloves. Mix constantly with a spatula and cook until onions are translucent. Lower and adjust heat as needed.

Once onions are slightly translucent, add graan masala and curry powder to onions and mix for a few seconds until spices are fragrant. Add curry sauce, and tomato puree and mix. Adjust heat to low and cover the pan, while the sauce simmers gently for about 2-3 minutes, stirring every so often. Add water or stock if the sauce gets too thick. Taste for seasoning and adjust as needed. Once 2-3 minutes have passed, add diced chicken into the sauce and mix to incorporate. Let chicken heat up for a...
Meanwhile, reheat 2 cups of Trifecta rice in the microwave. Divide evenly amongst all 4 plates, each with 0.5 cups.

recipes.title: Cookie Dough Protein Bar Recipe
recipes.description: Forget about snacks during your weekly meal prep? Whip up this bomb cookie dough protein bar recipe packing 10 grams of protein per serving.
ingredients: For the base of the bar:
3/4 cup Almond Flour (Bob's Red Mill recommended)
9 oz protein powder (Use a plant-based option to make these vegan)
1/2 cup of semi sweet chocolate chips
instructions: In the base of a food processor combine the oats, protein powder, almond flour, and dates, blend 1-2 minutes until combined thoroughly. Add all of the other ingredients, and pulse until the dough forms. Remove the dough from the mixer and fold in the chocolate chips using a spatula.

Line a sheet tray with parchment paper and spray oil with oil to create a non-stick surface. Transfer the almond butter-protein 'dough' to the bottom of the sheet pan. Spread out evenly to make an even layer bar. Add an extra 1/2 cup of chocolate chips if desired.
In a small non-stick saucepan heat the chocolate chips with 2 tablespoons of almond milk or milk of choice, and the coconut oil. Remove from heat and spread evenly on top of the cookie dough base.

Refrigerate the protein bar in the refrigerator for 10 minutes, or until the chocolate has set completely. To remove the protein bar from the pan, carefully separate the protein bar from the mold by cutting around the edges of the pan, making sure all sides are completely detached. Cut into 16 pieces.

recipes.title: Creamy Vegan Pasta Alfredo Recipe
recipes.description: Enjoy this dairy-free lavish and creamy 2 step vegan red-pepper alfredo sauce recipe paired with high-protein farfalle.
ingredients: 1 box chickpea or lentil pasta farfalle (or pasta of choice)
2 cups almond milk, soy milk, or hemp milk
½ cup roasted jarred piquillo peppers, drained
Pinch of salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Prepare pasta according to package instructions.
Combine cashews and alternative milk n a microwave-safe container. Microwave on high for 3 minutes, stop to stir, then microwave for an additional 2 minutes.
Blend warmed cashews and liquid with other ingredients, 2-3 minutes. Season with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Toss with pasta and any added vegetables, top with vegan parmesan, and toasted cashews.

recipes.title: Creamy Vegan Tikka Masala Sauce Recipe
recipes.description: This creamy and cozy dairy-free vegan tikka masala recipe will have you inspired to spice up all your go-to recipes.
instructions: First mince the ginger, garlic, and onion in a food processor to create a chunky paste. This will bring out all of the ingredients aromatic oils and enhance the flavor of the sauce. If you don’t have a food processor, sharpen up your knife skills and mince everything by hand with a chef’s knife; make sure to get the pieces as small as possible.

Heat a medium-sized pot or saucepan over medium-high heat and add 1 tablespoon of coconut oil then place in the minced aromatics. Stirring occasionally, cook the aromatics for 10 minutes until they begin to caramelize and brown.

Continuing to cook on medium-high heat, add your ground spices and cook for 1 minute until the spices are toasted slightly and you can smell a warm and rich bouquet of spices in the air. Stir in the diced tomatoes, and cook for 2 more minutes until the mixture begins to thicken. Add the stock and coconut milk and stir to release any more of the fond on the bottom of the pot then simmer for 5 minutes on medium-high heat.

Finish the sauce by mixing in the vegan yogurt and lemon juice then simmer for 2-3 more minutes until it has thickened slightly. Season with additional lemon juice, pinch of salt, and even a pinch of coconut sugar for a well-rounded and flavorful vegan tikka masala sauce.

recipes.title: Crispy Thai Steak Salad Recipe
recipes.description: A Thai steak salad recipe with a Thai-inspired peanut dressing and sesame marinated steak tossed with crunchy cruciferous vegetables, and refreshing herbs.
ingredients: 1 Thai chili pepper, minced or serrano pepper
2 cups Zucchini Noodles or Shaved Zucchini
1/4 cup cashews or almonds, roasted and chopped
instructions: Mix all ingredients for the marinade together in a small bowl then toss with cubbed steak and set aside for 4-5 minutes.
To prepare Thai Peanut dressing, mix all ingredients together in a medium bowl, and adjust with water if too thick.
Toss zucchini, slaw mix, and salad dressing together.
To cook steak, heat a skillet over medium-high heat, add oil and saute for 2-3 minutes until lightly browned and heated through. Remove from pan and plate with salad.
Top salad with toasted cashews and additional fresh herbs.

recipes.title: Delicious Asian Turkey Lettuce Wraps
recipes.description: A perfect balance between sweet and spicy, these asian turkey lettuce wraps are a great way to stay on track with your diet and have an absolutely delicious meal. Packed with protein and low in carbs, these wraps are something you don't want to miss out on!
instructions: In a pan, grill the Trifecta turkey burgers and mushrooms. Dice up the turkey burger using a spatula.
After grilling the turkey and mushrooms until lightly brown, add them to a bowl.
In the bowl, add the diced water chestnuts, siracha, honey, teriyaki sauce, and rice vinegar. Mix together.
Add the mixture to the lettuce leaves and top with peanuts, carrots, and bean sprouts.

recipes.title: Easy Steak Birria Tacos Recipe
recipes.description: Though we forego the classic consumé these birria tacos typically come with, the recipe for the sauce will let you wishing you could eat these every day!
ingredients: 16 ounces Trifecta flat iron steak, small diced into even squares
2 Guajillo chiles, dried, stem and seeds removed (¼ cup water saved)
instructions: Remove Trifecta flat iron steak and dice into small squares (think LA street tacos small dice cut).

Cut the onion in half and roughly chop one half. Add to a blender and keep aside for now. Then finely dice the other half and set aside in a small bowl. Rough chop the cilantro and add into the finely diced onion (this will be the garnish). Set aside.

Remove the stem of the chiles by cutting the top part. Once the top stem is cut, scoop out all seeds using the neck of a small spoon. It’s okay if chiles break. Remove as many seeds as possible. Add Anaheim chiles into a small saucepan and cover with water and bring to a boil. Reduce to a light simmer and simmer for 15 minutes. Remove from heat and leave covered for 5 minutes (this will steam the chiles further). Reserve ¼ cup of the boiling water and discard the rest.

Transfer the now boiled and rehydrated Anaheim chile to a blender with half of the roughly chopped onion. Add the 2 teaspoons of the pureed chipotle in adobo, ¼ cup of the boiling water, ¾ cup of beef stock, the garlic, avocado oil, and honey. Blend by starting to blend slowly, until chiles are pureed and the mix is smooth. Taste for seasoning and adjust if needed. Transfer to a medium bowl. You will have more puree than you need for this recipe.

Combine in a medium saute pan the diced flat iron steak, along with ¾ cup of the chile puree. Bring to a boil mixing constantly to heat through and then reduce the heat to a simmer and slightly reduce the sauce.
Meanwhile heat tortillas and keep warm. Heat over an open flame or in a microwave covered with a damp cloth towel. Keep warm until needed.
Cut lime wedges into quarters.
Place two tortillas per plate, and add 2 ounces of the warmed flat iron steak on each tortilla. Top with onion-cilantro mix. Serve with a lime wedge. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Fall Chicken Noodle Stir Fry
recipes.description: Sweet, savory, and a kick of spice, this butternut squash and chicken pad thai is the perfect meal! Loaded with protein and vitamin A, this meal is a great way to hit your macronutrients goal while still getting a variety of micronutrients! Better yet, it only takes about 5 minutes to make!
ingredients: 4oz Trifecta Chicken Breast, diced into squares
1/2 Cup of Butternut Squash Cubes, precooked
2oz of Brown Rice Pad Thai Noodles
instructions: First, cook the Pad Thai noodles as indicated by the package. Drain and set off to the side. Keep warm.
Heat up the pre-cooked butternut squash in the microwave. Cook for 3-5 minutes or until the squash softens.
Meanwhile, in a non-stick skillet, heat up the oil over medium heat, add the garlic, Trifecta chicken, and butternut squash. Cut up the chicken in the skillet and heat until golden.
Add the eggs to the skillet and scramble. As the noodles become 75%-80% cooked through, add the noodles to the skillet. Add the fish sauce, teriyaki sauce, and sriracha. Stir often.
Add the bean sprouts. Cook for an additional minute to two until bean sprouts are heated through. Serve on a bowl.
Top with red pepper flakes and cilantro.

recipes.title: High Protein Chia Seed Pudding Recipe
recipes.description: Learn how to prepare a simple, high-protein chia seed pudding recipe packed with flavor, fiber, and healthy fats.
ingredients: 1/2 scoop protein powder of choice (2-3 tablespoons)
instructions: For this recipe, you'll blend your berries, protein powder, lemon zest, vanilla, and optional sweetener with coconut milk in a blender until smooth.

Pour the berry mixture into the chia seeds and stir with a whisk or fork until the chia seeds are evenly distributed. Once you have combined your chia seeds with your base liquid, cover and store in the fridge for at least 2 hours until thickened or 8 hours overnight. Be sure to stir your pudding occasionally during this process as the chia seeds tend to settle to the bottom of your base liquid isn't very thick. This also prevents clumps of seeds from forming at the bottom or sides of the con...

Once your pudding has set top it with your favorite toppings like fresh fruit, granola, or coconut flakes and your chia seed pudding is ready to eat! You don’t have to add toppings, but it can add a ton of extra flavor, just be careful not to overdo it because a lot of extra toppings will alter the nutritional content.

recipes.title: High Protein Vegan Fudge Brownie Recipe
recipes.description: A drool-worthy high protein vegan fudge protein brownie recipe for a perfect chocolatey, rich, gooey-fudgy center, and 13 grams of protein per brownie.
ingredients: 1 ¼ cup protein powder (vanilla or chocolate)
2 teaspoon vanilla extract or almond extract
instructions: In a large bowl mix cacao powder, protein powder, almond flour, baking powder, and salt. You can use a vanilla or chocolate protein powder depending on how chocolatey you like your brownies.
In a small bowl combine the maple syrup, coconut oil, vanilla extract and dairy free milk until combined.
Blend together the wet and dry ingredients with a whisk or spatula until uniformly mixed. Divide batter evenly into a muffin tin sprayed with non-stick cooking spray. Bake for 22 minutes.
To prepare a quick vegan chocolate ganache, combine the cacao powder, coconut oil, vanilla extract, salt, maple syrup and dairy-free milk in a small bowl until smooth.

recipes.title: Honey Sriracha Turkey Meatballs
recipes.description: Sick of the same old meal prep week after week? This spicy and sweet combination is sure to reignite your meal prep flame. Tender and juicy turkey meatballs paired with light and fluffy brown rice, this is the meal you will look forward to throughout the week.
ingredients: 1/4 Cup of Pineapple (not for the inside of the meatballs)
instructions: In a large bowl, mix all of the ingredients for the turkey meatballs.
Roll meatballs into 1-inch balls. Place off to the side when rolled.

Heat up a skillet over medium. Grill the turkey meatballs on all sides, until each side is slightly browned. (Cover the skillet for 1-2 minutes to cook the inside of the meatballs before removing from heat).
In a small bowl, mix all of the ingredients for the sauce.
Dip the cooked meatballs into the sauce until thoroughly glazed in sauce.
Pair the meatballs with brown rice. Add in the pineapple. Top with sesame seeds and green onions. Add a lime for additional flavor.

recipes.title: Keto Avocado Egg Salad Recipe
recipes.description: This simple and healthy keto avocado egg salad recipe makes for the perfect high-protein snack or meal for any day of the week for your meal prep.
ingredients: 1/3 cup yogurt (can use dairy-free) or cottage cheese
instructions: You'll want to start with cooking the eggs since this will take a majority of the prep time.

Once your eggs have cooled, removed shells and chop them into small bite-size pieces, and set them aside - You can chop the eggs into whatever size you prefer, but smaller pieces typically tend to blend better with the other ingredients and provide better overall consistency. Use the grate trick, a fork, or knife to dice your eggs.

recipes.title: Keto BBQ Chicken Pizza with Cauliflower Crust
recipes.description: We have the perfect keto pizza recipe for you that is sure to cure the cravings and save you some calories and carbs!
ingredients: 1/2 Cup of G Hughes Sugar-Free Barbecue Sauce
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400F. Insert your round pizza pan or baking sheet in the oven and allow it to preheat along with the oven. Meanwhile, process your cauliflower in a food processor. If you bought a whole head of cauliflower, start by breaking down the cauliflower into even florets. Transfer the cauliflower to a bowl to hold, and then process in batches, making sure you get a fine cauliflower rice. Transfer the now processed cauliflower into a separate bowl, repeating the process until the e...

After the cauliflower is cooked, carefully remove it from the microwave, and spread it on top of a baking sheet lined with paper towels (have ready before removing bowls). For better results, add a few layers of paper towels at the base of the baking sheet, as this will help absorb as much moisture as possible. Spread the now-cooked cauliflower rice on the sheet tray and spread the cauliflower as evenly as possible. Using another set of paper towels, press these against the cauliflower and ta...

Carefully remove the now heated round pizza pan or baking sheet from the oven, using a cloth towel. Spray with spray oil and carefully transfer the dough from the parchment paper into the preheated sheet pan. Bake at 400F for 10 minutes. After the 10 minutes are done, remove from the oven, and add your toppings.

Once the 10 minutes are up, remove the cauliflower crust from the oven, and add the barbecue sauce as the base of the crust, followed by ¾ cup of mozzarella cheese, diced chicken, and sliced red onions. Return the pizza to the oven and bake for a final 5-7 minutes, until the sauce is bubbly and all ingredients are heated through. Remove pizza from the oven, slice with a pizza cutter, and serve immediately! Enjoy!

recipes.title: Keto Cauliflower Fried Rice
recipes.description: This recipe uses the famous keto cauliflower rice, for an easy low carb keto dinner experience. This recipe also qualifies as gluten free, and can also be vegan/vegetarian if chicken is substituted with tofu!
ingredients: 2 Stalks, green onion, sliced into rounds, green and white separated
instructions: Prepare the Cauliflower rice by cutting the cauliflower into florets and processing in a food processor until a grainy texture is achieved. Pulse for best results. Be careful not to process for too long, or the cauliflower will turn into mush.
Chop trifecta chicken breast into bite-size squares, maintaining an even cut to ensure even heating. Set aside. Rough chop trifecta eggs to add to the rice later on.

In sauté pan, over medium-high heat, heat 1 tbsp. of sesame oil and add garlic cloves and the white bulb of green onions. Lightly season with salt. Sauté until the onion and garlic begin to gain color. Immediately add the cauliflower rice.
Deglaze pan with tamari, and immediately add diced chicken and chopped eggs. Sauté until heated through.
Divide into two plates. Top with black sesame seeds, sliced bulb of green onions, and 1/4 avocado for each plate.

recipes.title: Keto Coconut Curry Sauce Recipe
recipes.description: This high fat sauce recipe is a deeply rich and flavorful keto coconut and spice-forward sauce, full of body and flavor.
ingredients: ¼ cup scallions, chopped small, white bulb
instructions: Prepare scallions, shallots, garlic, fresh ginger, fresh turmeric by combining minced shallots, garlic, and scallions in a bowl and grating turmeric and ginger into a separate bowl. Zest lime into a separate small bowl and keep on side. Heat coconut oil on a deep pot, until lightly smoking. Add vegetable mix and stir continuously, reducing the heat to low to avoid the veg mix from burning.

Once the mix is slightly caramelized, add the spice mix. Mix to fully incorporate. Immediately add the lime juice and deglaze the pan by scraping the bottom with a wooden spoon. Immediately after, add the coconut cream. Mix. Bring to boil once and reduce heat to an aggressive simmer.
Simmer over medium-low heat for 15 minutes to achieve a thicker consistency and develop flavor. Stir every 5 minutes.

Remove from heat and let cool slightly. Season with lime zest. Very carefully, with the lid ajar, blend in batches starting at a slow speed and increase speed slowly until sauce is fully blended. Strain. Transfer to and store in an air-tight container. Make sure the sauce is fully chilled before covering a container with a lid.

recipes.title: Keto Green Bean Casserole
recipes.description: Keto Green Bean Casserole is a perfect side throughout the year, but also a perfect side for your holiday meals, like Thanksgiving and/or Christmas. Be sure to take your sides to any party you attend so that you can also enjoy all the celebration and sharing with your loved ones while staying keto!
instructions: Blanch green beans for 2 to 3 minutes, as explained in the description above.
Cook bacon in the oven at 425F until crispy tender, on a foil-lined sheet tray. Cooldown and rough chop. Keep aside.
Mix slivered almonds and parm in a food processor, pulsing two to three times until fully incorporated. Set aside in a small bowl.

Dice shallots. Mix cream cheese, shallots, dijon, heavy cream, Worcestershire sauce, and tamari in a food processor. Season with salt and pepper. Make sure the mix is a running thick consistency. Adjust thickness as needed with water or additional cream.
In a large bowl, mix green beans and cream cheese mix. Add bacon and mix to incorporate.
Place a green bean mix in an oven-safe baking dish. Sprinkle almond/parm mix on top.
Bake in oven for 10 min. Change oven to broil setting at high, and broil until top of is golden brown.

recipes.title: Keto Strawberry Lavender Smoothie Recipe
recipes.description: You’ll love this playful keto strawberry lavender breakfast smoothie recipe made with fruity strawberries, cream cheese, chia seeds, and dried lavender.
ingredients: 3/4 cup coconut milk or almond milk
1 scoop vanilla or unflavored protein powder (optional)
instructions: Grab a blender, measure, and add all ingredients into the blender including the ice cubes.

Cover the blender with its lid, and blend for 30 seconds up to one minute - until everything is mixed well and a smooth consistency is created. Transfer your shake to your container of choice and repeat if making multiple shakes.

recipes.title: Keto Zhoug Sauce Recipe
recipes.description: A light yet highly flavorful keto Zhoug sauce recipe, similar to chimichurri but unique to its own essence.
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Prepare all ingredients as indicated. Combine all of them in a blender with the exception of the oil.

Before turning the blender on, begin pouring the oil slowly into the blender (remove the top or the middle plastic guard of the lid if one exists). Then turn on the blender and increase the speed to medium as you slowly continue to add the rest of the oil until all ingredients are pureed into an emulsified sauce.
Once all product is blended, taste for seasoning and adjust if needed. Also, feel free to add extra oil to yield a larger amount of sauce.
Transfer the sauce to a container and store in the fridge.

recipes.title: Korean Beef Bowl with Kimchi Recipe
recipes.description: Crumbled Trifecta beef patties tossed in a spicy, sweet and savory Korean-style sauce, served over white rice with a crunchy and sour punch of kimchi. Ready in less time than it takes to call for delivery.
instructions: Warm the beef patties in a microwave for about a minute. Then break them apart with your hands and set aside.
Chop scallions into small pieces, separating out 2 to 3 tablespoons of green ends for garnish.
In a small mixing bowl, combine liquid aminos, honey, vinegar and water.
Add sesame oil to a large saute pan and warm over medium-high heat.
Add in scallions and saute until onions cook through and start to brown.
Add shredded carrots to the pan and saute for another 1 to 2 minutes to soften.

Then add your beef crumble and sauce mixture, stirring everything together to mix well. Season with chili flakes and let cook for 3 to 5 minutes until the sauce begins to thicken and get absorbed by the beef mixture.
While your beef cooks, warm your rice in a microwave for 2 minutes.
Plate your bowl starting with the rice. Top with beef mixture and kimchi, and garnish with fresh scallions.
Enjoy!

recipes.title: Low-Carb Carne Asada Steak Taco Recipe
recipes.description: Love tacos but need to watch your carb intake? Enjoy this quick low-carb carne asada steak taco recipe prepared with a crunchy low carb jicama shell.
ingredients: Trifecta Meal Prep Flat Iron Steak
¼ cup cilantro with the stems, roughly chopped
2 teaspoon chili powder or smoked paprika
of your choice or pico de gallo
instructions: Combine all the marinade ingredients into a bowl and then place into a zip-lock baggie or container with the steak. Let marinade for 5 minutes at the least before cooking, for the best results marinade overnight or up to 2 days.

On the Grill or Grill Pan: Heat your grill to medium-high heat, brush the grates or grill pan with oil, and grill your Trifecta A La Carte steak for 1-2 minutes per side until charred and heated through.
Refer to alternative cooking methods above, saute or air-fryer if desired.
Remove your steak from the heat and let it rest for 1-2 minutes before slicing into it to preserve the juices. Once sliced, toss with extra marinade for a flavor boost.
Once you have prepared your steak, pick your toppings, and then fill your shell! I went with a simple homemade pico, fresh cilantro, and onion.

recipes.title: Low-Carb Pasta with Cashew Chicken Alfredo
recipes.description: With only 371 calories per PLATE, you won't believe how amazing this pasta tastes. Full of creamy, nutty, goodness, you will be addicted at first bite. All you need is a few ingredients, a blender, a skillet, 5 minutes, and you are ready to make this delight!
ingredients: 1 Pack of Tofu Shirataki Fettuccini Noodles
instructions: Using a blender, blend the cashews, almond milk, and nutritional yeast. Blend until smooth / moderately thick consistency is reached.
Then, rinse the Shirataki noodles a few times and dry.
On a cutting board, cut up the Trifecta Chicken into slices.
Heat a skillet up to medium temperatures. Spray with non-stick spray. Add the chicken and grill until slightly brown. Remove the chicken from heat.
Once all of the chicken has been removed, spray the skillet again with non-stick spray. Add the Shirataki noodles and cook for about a minute (make sure to move the noodles around).
Pour the cashew sauce into the pan. Mix the pasta around with the sauce for a few minutes. Add more almond milk if the sauce starts to thicken.
Add the chicken back into the pan. Mix. Then add the fresh basil and let the basil cook into the pasta.
Remove pasta from heat and serve. Top with red pepper flakes for a kick.

recipes.title: Macaroni and Beef Recipe
recipes.description: Macaroni and beef play a twist on a classic dish that everyone as a kid loved - add some veggies and make this recipe a healthy and hearty meal!
ingredients: 2 teaspoon grapeseed oil or preferred neutral oil
instructions: Cook pasta elbows as indicated by the box. Save ¼ cup of the starchy water.
Drain pasta over a colander and return elbow to the same pot. Sprinkle a small splash of olive oil into the pasta and mix to coat all elbows. Cover with lid and keep warm.
Crumble Trifecta Beef by hands into a bowl and keep on the side until needed.

In a large saute pan, heat oil over medium heat until the oil is slightly smoking, quickly for about 30 seconds, saute garlic and then immediately add the beef. Add tomato sauce, Worcestershire sauce, honey, broth. Mix to fully incorporate. Add the ¼ cup starchy water along with cherry tomatoes and the macaroni elbows. Mix, and bring to a light simmer, and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until warmed through. Taste for seasoning and adjust as needed. Be careful not to burn yourself when tasting food...
Get ready 4 plates. Carefully and evenly portion the macaroni and beef on the four plates.
Garnish with chives and cheese of preference. Enjoy!

recipes.title: Mahi Mahi Fish Taco Bowl
recipes.description: Easy fish taco bowls loaded with brown rice, a crunchy slaw tossed in a tangy, sweet and slightly spicy dressing, and topped with perfectly cooked fish and fresh salsa.
ingredients: 2 cups Green and purple cabbage, shredded
instructions: Add cabbage, carrots, and cilantro to mixing bowl and season with 1/2 of lime juice and a pinch of salt.
In another small bowl, blend yogurt, garlic, honey, 1/2 of the taco seasoning, and remaining lime juice. Add salt to taste.
Add yogurt mixture to veggies, stirring well to coat everything evenly.
Season pre-cooked fish with remaining taco seasoning and pan sear in 1 tablespoon of oil - about 1 to 2 minutes per side.
Warm brown rice in the microwave for 90 seconds while fish cooks.
To plate, layer ingredients starting with brown rice, followed by slaw, and fish, dividing ingredients evenly between four portions.
Top with pico de gallo and extra cilantro.
Enjoy!

recipes.title: Paleo Curried Chicken Salad Recipe
recipes.description: Spicy, sweet and easy to make paleo curried chicken salad. This twist on a traditional chicken salad recipe is sure to liven up your lunch or snack options this week!
ingredients: Portion out this salad into eight high protein snacks for the week or scoop out a larger portion and serve for lunch or dinner with the following h
Brown Rice or rice crackers to keep it paleo
A crisp side salad of mixed greens and veggies tossed in a light vinaigrette
instructions: Chop Trifecta chicken into bite-sized pieces and add to a large mixing bowl.
Chop scallions and add to bowl.
Add carrots and raisins.
In a separate small bowl, toss together mayo, apricot preserves, vinegar, and curry powder. Stir until evenly blended.
Season with salt and pepper.
Enjoy!

recipes.title: Paleo Roasted Spiced Nuts Recipe
recipes.description: A simple recipe for creating any kind of delicious paleo-friendly spiced nuts, a perfect snack for your meal prep!
instructions: Preheat your oven to 350F. In a bowl, measure both cups of cashew and almonds. Roast for 6 minutes. Remove from the oven and transfer slightly roasted nuts to a bowl. Decrease oven temperature to 300F.
In a separate bowl, whip egg whites to soft peaks. Meanwhile, gently melt the butter in a small saucepan. We used our lovely Breville Smart Oven.
Pour melted butter first, followed by maple syrup and chile powder, cinnamon powder, ginger powder, nutmeg, kosher salt, and cayenne pepper on top of the roasted nuts.
Slowly add the and whipped egg whites into the bowl, folding with a rubber spatula gently.
Transfer now-seasoned nuts to a lined baking sheet. Spread out evenly.

Bake nuts for 16 minutes. Set a timer for 8 and rotate the baking pan halfway through. Set a timer for an additional 8 minutes. Once the time is up, remove from oven, let cool to room temperature, and store in an airtight container. Enjoy 1 oz servings.

recipes.title: Protein No-Bake Peanut Butter Oatmeal Cookie Recipe
recipes.description: These no-bake protein peanut butter oatmeal cookies are a must-have in your weekly meal prep; satisfy your sweet tooth without blowing up your health goals
ingredients: 3 tablespoons almond milk or milk of choice
4 tablespoons peanut butter or nut butter
instructions: In a large bowl combine the oats, protein powder, cocoa powder, and coconut flakes. Do not mix in the chocolate chips yet.
Heat a small sauce pan or saute pan over medium heat and melt together the coconut oil, peanut butter, maple syrup, and half of the chocolate chips; about 1-2 minutes.

Combine the melted ingredients and mix until the cookie dough comes together, then add the rest of the chocolate chips. The dough should hold its shape when formed into a ball or cookie. Portion the cookies into 2 oz balls and press down slightly to form a cookie shape. Store in the fridge until you'd like to eat

recipes.title: Pulled Pork Nachos Recipe High-Protein
recipes.description: This pulled pork nachos recipe is a high-protein flavorful and satisfying meal that's perfect for meal prep or a quick dinner.
ingredients: 60 Chips or about 4.5 oz/132g Tortilla Chips
4 green onions, thinly chopped white and green parts
instructions: Make the zesty greek yogurt crema by mixing the greek yogurt with fresh lime juice in a small bowl until a creamy consistency is achieved. Add a pinch of salt if desired.

In 4 separate meal prep containers, place 1.15 oz or 33g of the tortilla chips, followed by 4 oz of the Trifecta pulled pork, ¼ cup of black beans, ¼ cup of low-fat Mexican-blend cheese, 2 tablespoons of tomato salsa, and a handful of chopped green onions and cilantro, along with 1 tablespoon of the zesty greek yogurt crema.
Top the containers with their lids and store in the fridge for up to 4 days!

If serving immediately, follow this entire process but make the loaded nachos in a parchment-lined sheet tray, briefly heat the pork before adding to the nachos, and melt the cheese in a broiler. Then top with the zesty crema and herbs. Enjoy

3 min
PREP TIME:
10 min
